title: SPARTA Arbitrage QA Harness Spec v1
research_objective: Define exactly how a FUTURE QA harness must check any future arbitrage dataset + per-dataset manifest BEFORE a research test is allowed to use it. This document does not fetch data, does not connect to any exchange, does not process datasets, does not run a backtest, and does not authorize trading. It is the written input spec that any future, separately-authorized QA harness implementation MUST satisfy.

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: A. Cross-exchange spot arbitrage
category_specific_qa_checks: venue_pair_clock_alignment_check: per-venue clock skew within max_skew_allowed
synchronized_l1_coverage_check: both venues have L1 at the same aligned timestamps
depth_at_test_size_check: configured test size <= cumulative depth on both sides
withdrawal_latency_assumption_attached_check: pointer to the data-contract row exists
fee_schedule_attached_check: per-venue fee tier dated and pinned
asset_network_compatibility_check: the asset+network pair is transferable across both venues

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: B. Spot-perp basis / funding arbitrage
category_specific_qa_checks: funding_timestamp_alignment_check: per-row settlement_ts aligned to documented venue UTC cutoff
funding_rate_completeness_check: rate, interval, settlement_ts non-null on every funding row
perp_contract_specs_attached_check: mark/index composition, tick, lot, multiplier non-null
liquidation_risk_notes_attached_check: maintenance margin + engine-model + partial/full behaviour documented
spot_perp_clock_alignment_check: spot rows aligned to perp mark/index timestamps within max_skew_allowed

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: C. Triangular arbitrage
category_specific_qa_checks: three_leg_pair_mapping_check: leg_1 / leg_2 / leg_3 declared with venue + symbol
snapshot_age_check: every snapshot's oldest leg <= max_snapshot_age_ms (default 250)
per_leg_taker_fee_attached_check: taker fee bps per leg present
per_leg_precision_check: tick_size + lot_size present per pair
per_leg_depth_check: configured test size <= depth per leg at the touch
leg_sequence_assumption_check: documented send order + per-leg time-to-fill present

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: D. Futures calendar / basis arbitrage
category_specific_qa_checks: expiry_calendar_present_check: per-contract last trading day + settlement date populated
contract_multiplier_present_check: multiplier per contract non-null
roll_calendar_present_check: which days the protocol would have rolled is explicit
margin_and_carry_assumptions_present_check: initial + maintenance margin + cost-of-carry per contract per venue documented
venue_source_pin_check: expiry/roll source citation matches the venue record

supported_arbitrage_categories.label: E. Statistical / relative-value mispricing (NOT pure arbitrage)
category_specific_qa_checks: aligned_bar_check: both legs share the same bar timestamp on every aligned row
spread_definition_attached_check: log-ratio / beta-adjusted / distance-from-mean explicitly recorded
is_only_diagnostics_check: cointegration / stationarity diagnostics were computed on IS window only (OOS unread)
regime_label_definition_check: if regime gating is used, the label generator is documented
not_pure_arbitrage_label_check: every downstream artifact carries the RELATIVE_VALUE label, not ARBITRAGE
corporate_action_adjustment_check: equities/ETFs carry raw + adjusted with a documented method

required_inputs: Pinned Arbitrage Data Contract v1 version reference.
Pinned Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 version reference.
QA configuration (max_skew_allowed_ms, max_snapshot_age_ms, depth_at_size, volume_filter, outlier_thresholds) -- all pre-declared, no auto-tuning during a run.
forbidden_inputs: Live exchange APIs at QA-run time.
Any source requiring an embedded API key, OAuth token, or .env credential at QA-run time.
Scraping any venue HTML / WebSocket / undocumented endpoint.
Synthetic / mock-priced data presented as historical (source_type 'synthetic_diagnostic_only_NOT_FOR_BACKTEST' is allowed only when QA explicitly logs it; it cannot reach QA_PASS).
Datasets whose OOS window was peeked before sealing.

A_manifest_integrity.purpose: Validate the per-dataset manifest exists, is well-formed, and pins the right versions BEFORE any row is inspected.
checks: required_manifest_fields_present: every field listed in arbitrage_dataset_manifest_v1.manifest_schema.required_future_manifest_fields is present and non-empty (except notes).
dataset_id_present: non-empty, canonical, never reused.
dataset_version_present: monotonically-increasing integer.
data_contract_version_referenced: explicit Arbitrage Data Contract version pin.
research_protocol_version_referenced: explicit Arbitrage Research Protocol version pin.
freeze_status_present: must be FROZEN before QA_PASS is possible.
qa_status_field_present_for_update: harness will populate qa_status; field exists on the manifest.
allowed_use_present: includes 'research_only'; must NOT include 'live_trading' / 'paper_order_execution' / 'broker_control' / 'exchange_connection'.
forbidden_use_present: explicitly forbids live_trading / paper_order_execution / broker_control / exchange_connection.
source_provenance_present: source_type, source_location, ingest_pipeline_version all non-null.
checksum_policy_present: sha256 (or stronger); per-file checksums verifiable on FROZEN.

B_timestamp_and_synchronization.purpose: Reject rows that violate the data contract's timestamp + synchronization rules.
checks: exchange_timestamp_exists: at least one of exchange_send_ts / exchange_recv_ts / venue_match_ts per row.
local_recv_ts_present_if_available: always recorded but never the sole timestamp.
timezone_normalized: stored timestamps are UTC; original timezone preserved as metadata.
max_skew_policy_enforced: rolling per-venue exchange_send_ts vs. local_recv_ts within max_skew_allowed.
stale_quote_policy: rows whose quote is older than max_skew_allowed + venue_publish_interval are flagged as stale.
duplicate_timestamp_policy: duplicates removed by (instrument_id, primary_ts, sequence_no_if_present); duplicates without a sequence number are INVALID.
monotonic_rule: primary_ts strictly monotonic per (instrument_id); OOO rows flagged.
cross_venue_alignment_rule: cross-venue / triangular tests share the same aligned timestamp source at sub-second resolution.

C_quote_and_spread.purpose: Reject quote-level anomalies and surface spread issues.
checks: bid_le_ask: best_bid <= best_ask on every L1 row.
non_negative_spread: best_ask - best_bid >= 0.
spread_outlier_detection: rows where spread > N * rolling_median_spread are flagged (default N documented in QA config).
stale_quote_detection: any quote older than max_skew_allowed at decision time is excluded.
quote_flicker_detection: recurring multi-tick flicker within a sub-second window is flagged.
missing_side_handling: row missing either bid or ask is INVALID.
crossed_market_handling: bid >= ask (crossed top-of-book) is flagged; excluded from MVT unless strategy explicitly handles it.

D_order_book_and_depth.purpose: Validate L1/L2 book quality and depth-at-size capability.
checks: depth_levels_present: L1 mandatory; L2 (>=5 levels) required if depth-at-size test is requested.
depth_at_size_calculation_requirement: cumulative size from best bid/ask up to the configured test size is documented per (venue, pair).
negative_or_zero_size_rejection: any level with size <= 0 is INVALID.
book_imbalance_warning: extreme one-sided book is flagged (configurable threshold).
stale_order_book_detection: L2 snapshot older than max_skew_allowed at decision time is excluded.
crossed_book_flag: best_bid level >= best_ask level is flagged.
trade_price_positive: price > 0 on every trade print.
trade_size_positive: size > 0 on every trade print.
outlier_print_detection: prints whose price deviates from rolling-mid by > M * spread are flagged.
quote_trade_alignment: trade venue_match_ts aligned with a contemporaneous quote book snapshot.
duplicate_trade_id_check: where the venue exposes a trade_id, duplicates are removed.
block_or_otc_print_flag: block / OTC prints are flagged separately from continuous-book trades.

F_fee_funding_cost.purpose: Make every PnL-shaping assumption explicit before any future test.
checks: maker_taker_schedule_present: maker_fee_bps + taker_fee_bps per (venue, instrument-class) non-null.
funding_timestamps_present_for_perps: spot_perp_basis_funding rows carry settlement_ts.
funding_rates_present_for_perps: spot_perp_basis_funding rows carry rate + interval.
withdrawal_deposit_fee_references_present: per asset per network non-null.
network_fees_reference_present: crypto on-chain gas distribution documented.
cost_fields_non_null_before_any_future_edge_claim: no future PnL number is allowed without fees + spread + slippage + funding/borrow lines.

G_liquidity_and_latency.purpose: Reject feeds where liquidity / latency assumptions cannot be honoured.
checks: minimum_notional_depth_check: per category per venue per pair threshold met.
volume_filter_check: pair-level minimum 24h volume threshold met (per QA config).
exchange_downtime_outage_flags_present: first-class boolean column exists (not inferred from gaps).
latency_skew_warning: rolling exchange_send_ts vs. local_recv_ts > max_skew_allowed triggers a WARN.
suspicious_venue_state_flag: documented categorical column populated when present (depth_collapse / spread_widening_spike / trade_print_without_book_change / post-outage_first_minute).

H_anomaly_detection.purpose: Detect anomalies whose presence either WARNs or FAILs QA.
checks: depth_collapse_detection: sudden zero-size at touch is flagged.
spread_widening_spike_detection: spread > K * rolling_median for X consecutive seconds is flagged.
trade_print_without_book_change_detection: prints lacking a contemporaneous book change are flagged.
post_outage_first_minute_detection: rows in the first minute after an outage flag are excluded from MVT unless strategy explicitly handles them.
regime_break_detection: structural breaks (statistical_relative_value only) are flagged for review.

manifest_validation_checks: Reject manifests whose source_location is a network URL.
Reject manifests with any safety flag True.
Reject manifests with freeze_status != FROZEN when QA is asked to run a research-grade pass.
Reject manifests where row_count_expected != row_count_actual without a documented gap.
provenance_qa_checks: every row carries source_id + data_contract_version + dataset_version
manifest_path resolves to an on-disk manifest file
normalization_qa_checks: currency_conversion documented; FX series cited if applied
tick/lot rounding documented; never silently rounded
split/dividend adjustment for equities documented; raw + adjusted both stored
category_specific_qa_checks: See per-category lists in supported_arbitrage_categories.

qa_status_rules.QA_DRAFT: QA harness has not yet been run on this dataset_version. Dataset is NOT research-usable.
qa_status_rules.QA_PASS: Every blocking check passed. Zero unresolved WARN-level issues OR every WARN has an explicit operator-acceptance note. row_count_expected == row_count_actual (or a documented gap explains it). Manifest is FROZEN with valid per-file checksums. Dataset is research-usable. QA_PASS does NOT imply profitability, does NOT imply edge, and does NOT authorize backtesting, paper trading, or live trading.
qa_status_rules.QA_WARN: Every blocking check passed, but at least one non-blocking warning is unresolved (e.g., borderline coverage / latency / liquidity / spread outliers / quote flicker). The dataset is only research-usable AFTER an explicit operator-acceptance memo per dataset_version. The acceptance memo must name the WARN, why it is acceptable, and the limit it imposes.
qa_status_rules.QA_FAIL: At least one blocking check failed (missing required field, broken checksum, crossed book on every row, manifest invalid, peeked OOS, etc.). Dataset is NOT research-usable until the cause is fixed and a new dataset_version is produced.
qa_status_rules.QA_BLOCKED: Externally blocked (legal / TOS / vendor restriction). Cannot proceed regardless of harness output.
qa_status_rules.QA_RETIRED: Dataset has been retired by the operator. QA history is preserved for traceability; cannot be re-promoted to QA_PASS.

qa_pass_does_not_imply: QA_PASS does NOT authorize paper-order execution.
QA_PASS does NOT authorize live trading.
QA_PASS does NOT authorize broker control or exchange connection.
QA_PASS does NOT promote any lane to ACTIVE or STRONG.
QA_FAIL BLOCKS research use of the dataset_version.
QA_WARN requires explicit operator-acceptance memo before any research use.

report_schema.description: The schema that every future per-run QA report must satisfy. The QA harness writes one report per (dataset_id, dataset_version) it inspects.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: All required top-level sections of this spec are present; all 8 QA check groups documented; all 5 categories carry category-specific checks; QA status model contains all 6 statuses; report schema lists all required future QA report fields; no safety flag is True; no forbidden phrase appears.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Spec is satisfied but at least one section is borderline (e.g., an anomaly threshold is loose); documented and re-checked before any reliance.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: Any required section is missing; any safety flag is True; any QA status is missing; any required future QA report field is absent; any forbidden phrase appears.

required_future_artifacts: QA harness implementation that satisfies this spec (separately authorized; standard-library or pinned-dependency only; no network at runtime).
Per-(dataset_id, dataset_version) QA report file satisfying report_schema.required_future_qa_report_fields.
Per-WARN operator-acceptance memo (when QA_WARN is accepted as research-usable).
Per-FAIL remediation memo OR new dataset_version that addresses the failure.
Retirement memo when QA_RETIRED transitions in.
safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No paper-order execution. No order placement.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this spec's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. No backtest in this bundle. No dataset processing in this bundle.
Do not modify paper / live execution files.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this spec alone.
A price gap is not profit. QA is not strategy validation. QA_PASS does not imply edge.
no_profit_claim_policy: A clean dataset does not imply profit.
A price gap is not profit.
No QA artifact authorizes live trading, paper-order execution, or broker control.
QA_PASS does not authorize backtesting; that is a separate, separately-authorized phase.
